opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've been to the toilet six times in the last hour passing mostly bright red blood and mucus. The cramping is agonising and I feel like I constantly need to go but nothing comes out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The blood suddenly increased tonight and I'm terrified I'm bleeding out.

symptom_narrative.character: frequent small volumes of bright red blood mixed with mucus; severe tenesmus and pelvic cramps
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonising cramps; frightened by the blood
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: small volumes each time (streaks/mucus, not cupfuls of dark blood/clots); mildly lightheaded from panic but no fainting on standing; no vomiting, no fever
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known an inflammatory bowel condition of the rectum (rectum only), usually mild

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an inflammatory bowel condition of the rectum (inflammation limited to the rectum)
past_medical_history.how_managed: topical mesalazine suppositories (ran out 2 weeks ago)

objective_data_offered.value: each time it's a small amount of bright red blood and mucus (not cupfuls of dark blood or big clots); I feel a bit lightheaded from panic but I don't feel faint when I stand up
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; cannot measure postural BP/HR or haemoglobin remotely
